subject: Re: [External]Following up from Chamber Connections
body:
Good morning Duncan,

I wanted to follow to see when you had time to chat or meet up.

Monday the 8th or Tuesday the 9th after 10:00 AM work for me.



DATA CLASSIFICATION | CONFIDENTIAL

Louis Eisenberg

[cid:flagler250x150_3fac1e38-c696-4504-8b59-12deb872acb0.png]


Louis Eisenberg
Branch Manager
Flagler Credit Union a Division of Dort Financial
Office 561-899-2532
Fax 561-432-2130
Louis.Eisenberg@dortfcu.org<mailto:Louis.Eisenberg@dortfcu.org>
<https://www.instagram.com/dortfinancialcreditunion>



CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: The contents of this email message and any attachments may contain confidential and/or privileged information legally protected from disclosure, and are intended solely for the recipient(s). If you are not the intended recipient of this message or their agent, or if this message has been addressed to you in error, please immediately alert the sender by reply email and then delete this message and any attachments. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, dissemination, copying, or storage of this message or its attachments is strictly prohibited.

________________________________

subject: =?UTF-8?q?=F0=9F=94=B4_For_subscriptions,_$8_is_$5?=
body:
----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3d56938f-f7ce-4784-919e-dab3c8df5287/image__39_.png?t=1748354774)
Follow image link: (https://creatorspotlight.com/)
Caption: Brought to you by beehiiv.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/f763b4bc-ba0d-43e6-a6dc-a66f1bc50cf6/image__194_.png?t=1779978528)
Follow image link: (https://pplx.ai/creator-spotlight-newsletter-1)
Caption: 

This week, I spoke to Stephen Totilo, one of the most experienced video games reporters working, about his two-and-a-half years as an independent creator-journalist.

**In this episode:**

* 📰 [Transitioning from employee to independent journalist](https://videos.creatorspotlight.com/v/ep113-stephen-totilo-4k?t=430)

* 💵 [Pricing your product as an independent journalist](https://videos.creatorspotlight.com/v/ep113-stephen-totilo-4k?t=1740)

* 📈 [Navigating the challenges of expanding a media business](https://videos.creatorspotlight.com/v/ep113-stephen-totilo-4k?t=2249)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/67deabec-c53a-4b6e-b502-6cd5c8f74b37/Group_20.png?t=1780401698)
Follow image link: (https://videos.creatorspotlight.com/v/ep113-stephen-totilo-4k?t=0)
Caption: 

**[Listen](https://www.creatorspotlight.com/podcast/s/tcsp/ep_113_how_he_makes_140k_writing_about_video_games_ft_stephen_totilo)** to the podcast | **[Watch](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9l6EbZ7ajy8)** on YouTube | Scroll down to **read** our profile.

_— Francis Zierer, Lead Editor_


----------



----------
## Everyone needs an editor — we built one for you

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/caa5bd8f-7957-46f0-942b-169b46988d32/Screenshot_2026-06-02_at_8.50.53_AM.png?t=1780404657)
Follow image link: (https://pplx.ai/creator-spotlight-newsletter-1)
Caption: 

The best way to use AI for writing is as an editing _partner_.

Especially if you don't have another person to edit your work and tell you what works and what doesn't.

When writing _Creator Spotlight_, we'll often paste our drafts into AI tools, asking what to cut, what feels repetitive, or what's just getting in the way. But … the advice we get back from these tools isn't often helpful.

In partnership with Perplexity Computer, we turned that workflow into a focused editing tool: [The Wordcount Terminator](https://pplx.ai/creator-spotlight-newsletter-1). The suggestions are specific, and in the right voice, so we can quickly see what actually needs to change instead of sifting through generic advice. And the Learn feature even rounds up great writing examples to help us keep learning and get sharper as an editor, not just faster.

(And if your problem is the opposite — your writing needs more instead of less — our tool can help with that too.)

[Give it a try](https://pplx.ai/creator-spotlight-newsletter-1).

_This is an advertisement._


----------



----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eb47a50c-ecc6-4903-9943-1c384b903960/IN_THE_SPOTLIGHT.png?t=1744661614)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1f8857b9-d641-41bf-84f5-ca29eae40538/image__196_.png?t=1780359179)
Follow image link: (http://creatorspotlight.com/p/stephen-totilo)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d06b7622-a275-476a-83f3-3947f79418de/Francis_Zierer__1_.png?t=1780063492) [By Francis Zierer, Lead Editor]
Follow image link: (https://www.linkedin.com/in/francis-zierer)
Caption: 

Stephen Totilo’s time was up.

His employer gave him four months’ notice; after that, they would no longer be able to fund his games newsletter. He had through December 2023 to figure out what came next.

Stephen had joined _Axios_ not three years earlier, after spending 12 years at _Kotaku_, one of the most popular video games publications on the internet. Vanishingly few journalists have covered this industry as long as Stephen, who got his start as a games reporter for _MTV_ in 2005.

When _Axios_ informed him of his impending unemployment, Stephen decided to try the solo creator journalist route.

He’d come close to it already in 2020, while still at _Kotaku_. A reporter at heart, after nearly a decade as Editor in Chief, he missed writing his own stories, which is part of why he decided _not_ to go independent at the time, being worried about name recognition after so long behind the scenes.

The _Axios_ opportunity came along shortly after, allowing Stephen to fulfill his desire to return to reporting without losing the safety and structure of traditional employment. 

By the time _Axios_ decided to shut Stephen’s newsletter down, he’d rebuilt that name recognition, with a loyal, thousands-strong audience reading his work every week. It was an amicable transition; he was allowed to promote his new venture on his way out. He announced _Game File_, his independent project, on December 13, 2023. By New Year’s Day, he already had 192 paying subscribers.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/febce6e3-a188-4d9a-9b24-8af21c486ca8/Content_Breaks__1_.png?t=1723054756)
Caption: 



## Two-and-a-half years as an independent voice

Stephen publishes one free and two paywalled issues per week. With over two decades of experience as a games reporter, he has a thorough network of sources and a deep well of knowledge; insiders trust him and come to him with tips.

One big feature last year saw him digging in legal files for details about a Star Wars video game superfan [suing a games company for false advertising](https://www.gamefile.news/p/star-wars-kotor-ii-lawsuit-saga) based on a four-second segment from a years-old teaser trailer. His connections also enable him to run, for example, [an interview](https://www.gamefile.news/p/my-kids-interviewed-playstation-about) between his nine-year-old twin children and the director of product communications for a PlayStation baseball game.

In [a post reflecting on ](https://www.gamefile.news/p/game-file-2024-report)**[one year of ](https://www.gamefile.news/p/game-file-2024-report)****_[Game File](https://www.gamefile.news/p/game-file-2024-report)_**, Stephen shared a few key metrics, including:

* Paid subscribers: 1,079

* Total subscribers: 17,000+

He wrote [another anniversary post](https://www.gamefile.news/p/game-file-year-two) to mark **two years of ****_Game File_**, this one published on January 23, 2026. This was another year of growth, though not as fruitful as the first:

* Paid subscribers: 1,400+

* Total subscribers: 25,000+

**In the four months since that post, the number of paid subscribers hasn’t changed much, but the **_**total**_** subscriber count has surpassed 27,000**. All told, Stephen has a solid business on his hands, currently generating over $140,000 in annualized revenue. But, [as he put it](https://www.gamefile.news/p/game-file-year-two#:~:text=That%20said%2C%20the%20number%20isn%E2%80%99t%20quite%20as%20good%20as%20it%20might%20initially%20look.%2010%25%20of%20that%20goes%20to%20Substack%2C%203%25%20to%20my%20payment%20processor%20Stripe%20and%20a%20healthy%20chunk%20goes%20to%20taxes.), “the number isn’t quite as good as it might initially look. 10% of that goes to Substack, 3% to my payment processor Stripe and a healthy chunk goes to taxes.”

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/febce6e3-a188-4d9a-9b24-8af21c48
